doctrine-check.reason: v1.1 correction: v1.0 of this DR was authored without reading governance/armature_v4.md, governance/population-taxonomy.md (CANONICAL), or DR-2026-06-11-remove-colonial-role, and independently re-derived much of the armature's two-layer architecture. v1.1 reconciles: it defers to the armature's vocabulary where stronger (mapping-confidence replaces flat roles), amends-by-proposal through population-taxonomy §5's own change process, realigns DB flat codes to canonical sub-codes (OFS/ME, NDV/AUT, NEU/PCS), preserves the canonical validator rules (DBL ≠ VIS+DEAF, BAR containment, ALL exclusivity), and withdraws the earlier session advice to populate lang_jur_map (deferred pending owner criteria per DR-2026-06-11). The DR remains PROPOSED and states-without-executing per the posture DR.

source-discipline.reason: Factual claims are corpus-verification claims checked in-session against the live database and repo files, with the session's own instrument errors (a silent join failure; a regex false positive that manufactured a b-code) corrected in the record. v1.1 adds the standing caveat that absence checks were run with English terms over an EN-heavy corpus, and gates the register behind a non-English concept-validation pass (§9.2). All literature-dependent content remains deferred: nothing in the doctrine is citable evidence, every axis carries a falsification condition, and no threshold may enter an item without tier-anchored sourcing.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: PI rule #11 attestation obligation; re-grounded for DR v1.1 at doctrine 0f2f525 with the biases disclosed below.

bias_direction: The agent authored the critique, the taxonomy, both adversarial passes, the amendments responding to them, and this attestation — a closed loop in which the amendments could flatter the critiques rather than the truth. Expansion bias remains documented (17 axes, 7 new profiles). New in v1.1: canon-reconciliation could over-credit convergence with armature_v4 as validation when both could share a common blind spot (both are solo/AI-adjacent artifacts; neither has had lived-experience review — which is why the Co-1 review gate is blocking, not decorative). Bounded by: PROPOSED status; staged SQL outside the migration glob, validated only against a scratch copy; symmetric falsification conditions permitting collapse; two blocking gates (Co-1 review, non-English validation) that ratification schedules rather than discharges; explicit ratification items (R6-R8) where this DR and the armature diverge, adjudicated by the owner, not by the agent.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue v1.1 performs sensitivity rather than achieving it: the same solo AI process that produced the criticized language now certifies its own corrections, adversarial passes included, and no disabled person has reviewed any of it — making the Co-1 gate a promissory note from a debtor with no current means to pay. Response: the promissory structure is the mission's own declared operational reality (§Operational reality: partial CRPD Art. 4.3 pre-launch, honestly calibrated, with solo-permanent named as a possible end-state); the amendments are anchored to canon that predates this session (armature §5's interaction framing, carer role, Deaf-identity open question) rather than to the agent's taste; the gates are recorded as blocking in the ratification checklist so the owner cannot ratify past them silently; and the register's anti-fig-leaf rule makes 'disclosed but unbounded' a findable non-compliance at doctrine-recheck rather than a rhetorical escape.
